etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "emasculate" from Latin "emasculare" (to deprive of masculine vigor)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Deprived of masculine vigor; experiencing diminished powerlessness and emasculated weakness supported by empowerment awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Masculine deprived → diminished powerlessness → contemporary emasculated weakness and emasculated diminishment that benefits from strength growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Masculine deprived → diminished powerlessness → emasculated weakness → strength understanding
